name: npm (GitHub)
description: npm (GitHub) — JavaScript package registry for publishing, discovering, and managing Node.js packages via CLI.

seo.title: npm (GitHub) — CI/CD for Applications
seo.meta_description: npm (GitHub): npm (GitHub) — JavaScript package registry for publishing, discovering, and managing Node.js packages via CLI.

when_to_use: Publish and install Node.js packages via npm CLI
Private npm registry for internal package distribution
Automated version publishing in CI release pipelines
Manage package scopes and access controls for teams

pros: The default registry for all of JavaScript
Free publishing and unlimited public packages
cons: Recurring supply-chain attacks on popular packages
Typosquatting and namespace pollution are constant
Feature pace slowed since GitHub acquisition
review.verdict: You don't choose npm, you inherit it — secure your installs with lockfiles and auditing.
review.best_for: Any JavaScript or TypeScript project, public or private.
review.not_for: Teams who want stronger provenance; look at JSR or pnpm-only workflows.

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Freemium tier, no explicit peak-lock evidence
